title: Death in the Imperial City: U.S. Marines in the Battle for Hue, 31 January to 2 March 1968
author_or_org: Richard D. Camp / Marine Corps University
geographic_scope: Huế city and approaches, Thừa Thiên Province
strengths: Concise official synthesis with page-level numeric claims on population, allied dead, enemy strength, and overall civilian devastation.
limitations: Civilian mortality is reported as 'dead or missing' rather than a clean direct-death series; enemy KIA range relies partly on body counts; side attribution for aggregate civilian deaths remains incomplete.
notes: Core anchor source for Huế battle dates, population baseline, allied dead, enemy participants, and top-line civilian destruction.

title: The 1968 Tet Offensive Battles of Quang Tri City and Hue
author_or_org: Erik Villard / U.S. Army Center of Military History
geographic_scope: Huế city and approaches, northern I Corps
strengths: Detailed operational narrative with force-presence clues, refugee/homeless counts, and captured-document evidence on executions/roundups.
limitations: Not a victim registry; some atrocity-related claims are contextual rather than status-clean civilian series.
notes: Important for theater mapping, early-force presence, refugee evidence, and side-attribution context.

title: The Viet-Cong Strategy of Terror
geographic_scope: Huế and surrounding grave-find areas, Thừa Thiên Province
strengths: Provides the most granular publicly accessible breakdown of post-battle casualty and grave-find accounting.
limitations: Strongly polemical framing; at least one identified grave list includes 25% military victims, making the grave-find totals unsuitable as a clean civilian-only core numerator.
notes: Useful for auxiliary casualty/injury series and for explaining why some apparent civilian totals should not be ingested uncritically.

title: The 1968 'Hue Massacre'
pages_or_sections: Part Two, section 'Feed Them a Number...'
strengths: Valuable for source genealogy: identifies the provincial Social Services estimate of 3,776 civilians killed in fighting and challenges later political-warfare arithmetic.
limitations: Explicitly argumentative and not itself a complete casualty registry; should be used as a provenance and lower-bound critique rather than sole truth source.
notes: Key for explaining disagreement between battle-death and massacre/dead-or-missing narratives.

title: U.S. Involvement in the Vietnam War: The Tet Offensive, 1968
author_or_org: Office of the Historian, U.S. Department of State
strengths: Concise official summary of Tet phases and why urban/offensive windows should be treated separately.
limitations: Too high-level for Huế numerator or denominator estimation.
notes: Used for conflict-family framing and episode splitting rationale.

title: Vietnam War
strengths: Compact, current reference summary of the conflict's broad dates, participants, and regional scope.
limitations: Not episode-specific and not suitable for Huế numerators/denominators.
notes: Used only for top-level conflict-family context.

title: Vietnam War Campaigns
author_or_org: U.S. Army Center of Military History
strengths: Official campaign timeline with a useful battalion-count snapshot for Huế and exact campaign date anchors.
limitations: Campaign summary, not a fine-grained casualty or population source.
notes: Used to support episodeization and to sanity-check Huế force-presence modeling.

title: History of the Vietnam War
strengths: Useful official date anchor for An Lộc and other episode candidates.
limitations: Very brief; unsuitable for numerator/denominator work.
notes: Used only for proposed episode timing.

title: Veteran Remembers Battle of Khe Sanh
strengths: Official source with clear siege dates and duration.
limitations: Commemorative article, not an analytical casualty source.
notes: Used only to anchor the proposed Khe Sanh episode window.

title: Linebacker II
author_or_org: Rebecca Grant / Air & Space Forces Magazine
strengths: Clear summary of the Linebacker II bombing window and strike geography.
limitations: Not a denominator or casualty registry for civilian-risk estimation.

title: Fall of Saigon
strengths: Clear date anchor for the final Saigon-collapse episode.
limitations: Not suitable for detailed DR estimation.
